title: Valid Palindrome
description: Check if a string reads the same forwards and backwards after removing all non-alphanumeric characters and converting to lowercase.

examples.input: "A man, a plan, a canal: Panama"

hints: Use two pointers moving inward from both ends.
Skip non-alphanumeric characters before comparing.

title: Remove Duplicates from Sorted Array
description: Given a sorted array, remove duplicate elements in-place and return the count of unique elements.
hints: Use a slow pointer to track where unique elements go.
Advance fast pointer through the array.

title: Merge Sorted Array
description: Merge two sorted arrays into the first array, which has enough space to hold both.
hints: Start filling from the end to avoid overwriting.
Compare elements from the back of both arrays.

title: Valid Palindrome II
description: Check if a string can become a palindrome by removing at most one character.
hints: Use two pointers from both ends.
When mismatch occurs, try skipping either left or right character.

title: Two Sum II - Sorted Array
description: Find two numbers in a sorted array that add up to a target. Return their 1-based indices.
hints: Use two pointers at start and end.
Move left pointer if sum is too small, right if too large.

title: 3Sum
description: Find all unique triplets that sum to zero.
hints: Sort the array first.
Fix one element and use two pointers for the rest.
Skip duplicates to avoid duplicate triplets.

title: 3Sum Closest
description: Find three numbers whose sum is closest to a target value.
hints: Sort and use fixed pointer with two moving pointers.
Track the closest sum seen so far.

title: Container With Most Water
description: Find two lines that together with the x-axis form a container holding the most water.
hints: Area is min(height[left], height[right]) * distance.
Start with widest container and move the shorter line inward.

title: 4Sum
description: Find all unique quadruplets that sum to a target value.
hints: Sort and use two nested loops with two pointers.
Skip duplicates at each level.

title: Trapping Rain Water
description: Calculate how much water can be trapped between bars after rain.
hints: Water at each position = min(maxLeft, maxRight) - height.
Use two pointers tracking left and right maximums.
